assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable that creates a surviving consolidated foreign currency exposure is most directly analysed under the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a cash flow hedge model for foreign currency risk on recognised items, making it the other hedge accounting treatment to consider.
